name: Fluent Bit Log Processor API
what_it_does: Ultra-lightweight open-source log processor and forwarder written in C — collects, parses, filters, and routes logs and metrics from any source to any destination, with an HTTP monitoring API for health checks and pipeline introspection.
use_cases: Collecting and forwarding logs from containers, VMs, and edge devices to centralized observability platforms
Parsing and enriching log streams with Lua filters before routing to Elasticsearch, Loki, or S3
Monitoring Fluent Bit pipeline health and throughput via HTTP monitoring API
Lightweight telemetry agent for Kubernetes DaemonSet deployments with minimal CPU/memory footprint
Edge IoT log collection with buffering and retry for intermittent connectivity
not_for: Full log aggregation and storage (use Loki, Elasticsearch, or Graylog for that)
Complex CEP (Complex Event Processing) or stream processing (use Flink or Kafka Streams)
Teams needing a hosted managed logging pipeline (use Datadog or Splunk)
best_when: An agent or pipeline needs to collect, transform, and route logs/metrics from many sources to many destinations with minimal resource consumption and maximum throughput.
avoid_when: You need log storage, querying, or visualization — Fluent Bit is a forwarder/processor, not a backend.

auth.notes: HTTP monitoring API has no auth by default — intended for local/internal use. TLS and basic auth can be enabled. Self-hosted: auth management is operator responsibility. Upstream destinations have their own auth (TLS, tokens, etc.).

pricing.paid_tiers: Calyptia (Chronosphere) provides commercial support and Calyptia Core managed pipeline product
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source. CNCF graduated project. Part of the Fluentd ecosystem. Calyptia (now part of Chronosphere) provides commercial Fluent Bit support. Can run in 450KB RAM — designed for embedded/edge.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; limited by hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP monitoring API exposes /api/v1/health, /api/v1/metrics, /api/v1/plugins, and /api/v1/uptime. JSON responses for pipeline introspection. Configuration is file-based (not API-driven) — agents can read metrics but cannot reconfigure pipelines at runtime.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Monitoring API is read-only — all GETs are fully idempotent. Configuration changes require restart.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP API is monitoring-only — cannot change pipeline config at runtime via API
Configuration is file-based (YAML/classic config) — restart required for config changes
No authentication on monitoring API by default — must be network-restricted in production
Plugin ecosystem is C-based — custom filters require Lua scripting or Go plugins (Wasm experimental)

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable C codebase. CNCF graduated project with security reviews. Monitoring API unauthenticated by default — requires operator hardening. TLS configurable. Upstream connections support mTLS and token auth. No formal certifications (self-hosted).
